item.title: News
item.description: Stay up to date on new cybersecurity solutions, as well as BI.ZONE news and events

item.title: BI.ZONE sheds light on data breaches caused by Leak Wolf’s malware-free attacks
item.description: The Leak Wolf group hacks Russian companies and publishes their data in its Telegram channel. The so-called hacktivists use no malicious software and act under the guise of company employees to evade detection

item.title: BI.ZONE releases in-depth guidelines on business impact analysis
item.description: Readers will have step-by-step instructions on how to run a business impact analysis (BIA) in their organizations along with practical recommendations on ways to boost cyber maturity

item.title: BI.ZONE presents an updated version of its antifraud system
item.description: BI.ZONE AntiFraud is an adaptive cross-channel system designed to protect against fraud. It provides transaction and session analysis with threat intelligence from a wide range of sources. The product is suitable for both banking and online services

item.title: BI.ZONE unearths SEO poisoning attacks targeting accountants
item.description: The Watch Wolf hacker group steals money from Russian companies by planting malware on their accountants’ computers. Its approach is far from what one might expect: instead of tried and true phishing emails, the group is betting on search engine optimization (SEO) to promote fraudulent websites

item.title: BI.ZONE CESP swiftly responds to protect Outlook users against CVE-2023-23397
item.description: Adversaries are now exploiting a new Microsoft Outlook vulnerability to steal user credentials. BI.ZONE CESP protects the users who can't update the email client

item.title: BI.ZONE detects destructive ransomware attacks against Russian users
item.description: Earlier this month, BI.ZONE Cyber Threat Intelligence team detected an attack campaign engineered by the Key Wolf hacker group

item.title: BI.ZONE specialists are now certified to latest ISO/IEC 27001:2022
item.description: This certification will enable BI.ZONE to prepare its clients for the transition to the new standard and assist them in organizing their security processes accordingly

item.title: BI.ZONE launches Cyber Polygon platform for individual trainings
item.description: The company has unlocked its free Cyber Polygon technical training platform. All tasks are now available to individual cybersecurity practitioners and can be accessed 24/7 and as many times as needed

item.title: Save the date: OFFZONE 2023 is coming in August
item.description: The 4th OFFZONE international conference on practical cybersecurity will take place in Moscow on August 24–25, 2023

item.title: New BI.ZONE service helps to detect signs of intrusions
item.description: BI.ZONE Compromise Assessment is designed to uncover ongoing and past attacks by analyzing corporate infrastructure

item.title: BI.ZONE CESP reinforced to improve email protection
item.description: Changes include improvements to the filtering system: now phishing links and malware do not have a chance to remain unnoticed even when hidden in password-protected attachments

item.title: BI.ZONE Brand Protection revamped to better protect business from cyber threats
item.description: We have released a new version of the BI.ZONE Brand Protection platform. In the first six months of 2022, the solution helped to detect 4 times more data leaks year-on-year

item.title: The number of fraudulent websites in Q1 2022 triples compared to 2021
item.description: During the first quarter of 2022, BI.ZONE blocked a total of 16,311 malicious sources, which is three times more year over year

item.title: Cyber Polygon postponed
item.description: The next event date will be announced later

item.title: Presenting an updated BI.ZONE AntiFraud solution
item.description: The new offline testing and advanced processing features will help more effectively detect both known and new fraudulent schemes as well as reduce the load on the hardware

item.title: Deception module and macOS support: BI.ZONE EDR functionality expanded
item.description: The Deception module allows you to track down even a professional attacker capable of evading detection at the initial stage of reconnaissance. Key EDR features are now available not only on Linux and Windows, but also on macOS

item.title: BI.ZONE: threat actors use leaked source code to attack Russian companies
item.description: The criminal groups Battle Wolf, Twelfth Wolf, and Shadow Wolf are targeting Russian organizations with the popular ransomware programs Babuk, Conti, and LockBit, which leaked to the public domain. BI.ZONE Cyber Threat Intelligence has recorded more than 40 attacks

item.title: Fourth OFFZONE conference on practical cybersecurity held in Moscow
item.description: In the two days of the event, 108 cybersecurity experts spoke to a crowd of over 2,500 people. The conference was supported by 50 partners, including 18 media outlets and 15 cybersecurity community groups

item.title: Teams from 117 countries demonstrated ethical hacking skills in CTFZone 2023
item.description: Hackers from Russia, China, Czechia, France, Indonesia, and South Korea made it to the finals. Four of the finalists belong to the world's top 10 CTF teams

item.title: BI.ZONE discovers new phishing campaign distributing White Snake stealer to Russian companies
item.description: Experts from BI.ZONE Cyber Threat Intelligence have discovered a phishing campaign targeting Russian organizations. The White Snake stealer—malware that steals passwords and other data from infected devices—is distributed under the guise of legitimate notifications from the the Russian Federal Service for Supervision of Communications, Information Technology and Mass Media (Roskomnadzor)

item.title: BI.ZONE CESP enables customized protection of corporate email
item.description: Now users can configure more than 20 parameters to filter their incoming and outgoing correspondence

item.title: BI.ZONE Secure SD-WAN update delivers network scalability and security improvements
item.description: BI.ZONE Secure SD-WAN is a Russian platform for building a secure network infrastructure between geographically distributed branches. Changes in version 1.4.0 simplify routine processes and network scaling, improve security, and expand the model range of equipment compatible with the platform

item.title: Cybercriminals debut Russian remote access software for targeted attacks
item.description: The BI.ZONE Cyber Threat Intelligence team has detected attacks on the hospitality industry by a new group called Quartz Wolf. This is the first time a Russian remote access solution has been used against local companies. This is how criminals bypass conventional defenses and successfully gain a foothold in the business infrastructure.

item.title: Red Wolf is back to spy against commercial firms
item.description: BI.ZONE has detected a new wave of attacks by the Red Wolf group (aka RedCurl) that went off the radar in 2022

item.title: Surge recorded in websites offering quick earnings on Tesla
item.description: Since the beginning of 2023, BI.ZONE has identified almost 3,000 fraudulent domains imitating the website of a Tesla X investment project

item.title: BI.ZONE contributes to the International Committee of the Red Cross on the digitalization of humanitarian emblems
item.description: For two years, the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) has been researching the risks of implementing a digital red cross emblem

item.title: BI.ZONE uncovers espionage attacks on the defense industry and critical infrastructure
item.description: The Core Werewolf group takes advantage of legitimate software and phishing to gain control over the victim system, copy the files, and track the user's activities. The threat actors target confidential information about Russia's critically important facilities

item.title: BI.ZONE: cybercriminals steal corporate passwords using open‑source software
item.description: BI.ZONE threat intelligence experts uncovered a malicious campaign targeting Russian organizations from different industries. It is aimed at spreading Umbral Stealer, which collects user credentials from infected computers. Note that the malware source codes are publicly available to anyone on GitHub, an internet hosting service for software development

item.title: Fluffy Wolf causes much damage with unsophisticated attacks
item.description: The malware-as-a-service model paves the way for cybercriminals with a shallow grasp of technology. Easily available and affordable, such solutions drive down the cost of a cyberattack while social engineering ploys heighten the chance for its success
